SpruceX Reactive Blueprint Framework
Documentation Examples Download

Contents

1. Introduction 2. Attributes Reference 3. SPA Navigation 4. Auto-Animate 5. Holiday Themes 6. Plugins 7. LLM.txt

1. Introduction

SpruceX is a lightweight HTML extension library that brings reactivity, state management, and modern features like SPA navigation and DOM morphing directly to your markup. It sits nicely between vanilla JS and heavier frameworks.

These docs track the 1.0.0 release line. SpruceX is still in active development and is not complete yet, so examples and APIs may continue to evolve.

<script src="/dist/sprucex.js"></script> <div sx-data="{ count: 0 }"> <button sx-on:click="count++">Increment</button> <span sx-text="count"></span> </div>

2. Attributes Reference

Core State & Binding

Attribute Description
sx-data Defines the component scope with an object literal or function.
sx-text Evaluates an expression and sets the element's textContent.
sx-html Evaluates an expression and sets the element's innerHTML.
sx-show Toggles the element's visibility (display: none) based on truthiness.
sx-bind:<attr> Binds an attribute value to a JS expression (e.g., sx-bind:src="url").
sx-class Conditionally applies CSS classes based on an object or string.
sx-model Two-way data binding for inputs. Modifiers: .number, .trim, .lazy, .debounce-ms.
sx-ref Registers the element in $refs with the given name.
sx-toggle Toggles a boolean property in the state on click.
sx-init-data Embedded JSON script for identifier-based sx-data roots.
sx-local Persists component state to localStorage using the value as the key.

Logic & Flow Control

Attribute Description
sx-for Iterates over an array (e.g., item in items) on a <template>.
sx-memo Memoizes sx-text or sx-html based on a dependency array.

Event Handling

Attribute Description
sx-on:<event> Attaches an event listener. Modifiers: .prevent, .stop, .self, .window, .document, key names.

Network & Requests

Attribute Description
sx-get/post/put/delete Defines the URL and method for the request.
sx-trigger Event that triggers the request (default: "click", forms use "submit"). Supports modifier syntax like input.debounce.300.
sx-trigger-debounce Debounces request triggers by the provided milliseconds.
sx-target CSS selector indicating where to swap the response.
sx-swap Swap method: innerHTML, outerHTML, before, after, prepend, append, morph.
sx-vars Object defining variables for the URL template.
sx-body Request body from expression/object/string.
sx-body-type Body serializer strategy: json or form.
sx-headers Expression object that maps request headers.
sx-include Selector for other inputs/forms to include in the request body.
sx-loading-into State target toggled true/false during the request lifecycle.
sx-error-into State target for serialized request errors.
sx-disable-while-request Disables form controls while the request is in flight.
sx-text-while-request Temporary button text while request is pending.
sx-confirm Confirmation message before request, including native form submit.
sx-cancel-previous Aborts the previous in-flight request for the same binding before starting a new one.
sx-json-into Parses JSON response into the specified state path.
sx-optimistic Expression to update state immediately before request.
sx-revert-on-error Expression to revert optimistic update if request fails.
sx-poll Polling interval in milliseconds.
sx-poll-while Expression that must be true for polling to continue.

Navigation & Performance

Attribute Description
sx-page Marks the container for SPA navigation updates.
sx-boost Enables link prefetching on hover/focus.
sx-boost-on Events to trigger boost (default: "mouseenter").
sx-lazy Delays component init until it enters the viewport.
sx-animate Auto-animates children using @formkit/auto-animate.
sx-preserve Prevents element from being modified during morphing.
sx-key Unique key for identifying elements during lists/morphing.

Miscellaneous

Attribute Description
sx-error-fallback Content to display if an expression error occurs.
sx-chart Declarative Chart.js binding with managed init/update/destroy.
sx-chart-type Explicit chart type override (e.g. line, bar).
sx-chart-options Expression object forwarded to Chart.js options.
sx-gridstack Initializes a GridStack instance from an options expression.
sx-gridstack-options Additional GridStack options expression.
sx-gridstack-option:<name> Per-option GridStack attribute mapping.
sx-gridstack-on-change / ... Writes GridStack event payloads into state targets.

Early Bootstrap Hook

Define window.SpruceXBoot.initTheme() before loading SpruceX to toggle theme classes before deferred component init.

window.SpruceXBoot = { initTheme() { ... } }

3. SPA Navigation

How it works

Add sx-page to your main content container. SpruceX will intercept all internal links, fetch the new page, and morph the content inside that container without a full reload.

History API DOM Morphing

Turbo Boosting

Add sx-boost to links or their containers to prefetch pages on hover, making navigation instant.

sx-boost sx-boost-on="mouseenter focus" Pre-fetching Cache aware

Events

Listen to sprucex:page-before and sprucex:page-after on the document to show loading bars or trigger analytics.

document.addEventListener('sprucex:page-before', ...)

4. Auto-Animate

Zero-Config Animations

Just add sx-animate to a parent element.

  • Automatic layout transitions
  • Smooth enter/leave animations
  • Works with sx-for and sx-show

Configuration

Pass a JSON object to sx-animate to customize.

  • sx-animate="{ duration: 500 }"
  • sx-animate="{ easing: 'ease-out' }"
  • sx-animate="{ duration: 200, easing: 'linear' }"

5. Holiday Themes

Keep the SVG static, hide decorations by default, and toggle a parent class based on date. This keeps markup clean and the behavior predictable.

Base Logo

Holiday Variant (Dec-Jan) 

<div id="logo-container">
  <svg viewBox="0 0 128 128" aria-label="SpruceX logo">
    <!-- base tree -->
    <path d="M64 16 L82 42 L74 42 L90 64 L80 64 L94 88 L34 88 L48 64 L38 64 L54 42 L46 42 Z" />
    <rect x="58" y="88" width="12" height="14" rx="2" />

    <!-- holiday-only details -->
    <g class="holiday-decorations">
      <polygon points="64,6 67,14 76,14 69,19 71,28 64,23 57,28 59,19 52,14 61,14" />
      <circle cx="64" cy="36" r="3" />
      <circle cx="52" cy="55" r="3" />
      <circle cx="76" cy="55" r="3" />
      <circle cx="64" cy="75" r="3" />
    </g>
  </svg>
</div>

<style>
  .holiday-decorations { display: none; }
  .holiday-theme .holiday-decorations { display: block; }
</style>

<script type="module">
  function applyHolidayTheme(containerId = "logo-container", date = new Date()) {
    const month = date.getMonth();
    const isHolidaySeason = month === 11 || month === 0; // Dec or Jan
    document.getElementById(containerId)
      ?.classList.toggle("holiday-theme", isHolidaySeason);
  }

  applyHolidayTheme();
</script>

6. Plugins

Writing a Plugin

Register plugins with SpruceX.integration(name, integration). Each integration can provide hooks to set up, scan elements, update UI, and clean up.

  • setup(component) initializes plugin state.
  • scan(component, el) collects directive bindings.
  • update(component) applies changes on initial render and reactive updates.
  • teardown(component) removes listeners/timers and clears plugin state.

Minimal Integration Example 

<script>
  SpruceX.integration("uppercase", {
    setup(component) {
      component._uppercaseBindings = [];
    },
    scan(component, el) {
      const expr = el.getAttribute("sx-uppercase");
      if (!expr) return;
      component._uppercaseBindings.push({ el, expr });
    },
    update(component) {
      (component._uppercaseBindings || []).forEach(({ el, expr }) => {
        const value = component.evaluateExpressionOrLiteral(expr);
        el.textContent = String(value ?? "").toUpperCase();
      });
    },
    teardown(component) {
      component._uppercaseBindings = [];
    },
  });
</script>

7. LLM.txt

LLM.txt

Download LLM.txt

Machine-readable runtime and plugin reference exposed at /LLM.txt. 

SpruceX LLM.txt
Version: 1.0.0
Status: Active development, not complete yet.

Overview

SpruceX is a lightweight reactive HTML framework that combines Alpine-style
declarative state with HTMX-style network actions. It uses `sx-*` attributes on
plain HTML and auto-initializes on `DOMContentLoaded`.

Install / Entry Points

- Browser bundle: `/dist/sprucex.js` or `/dist/sprucex.min.js`
- Package entry points: `lib/sprucex.mjs`, `lib/sprucex.cjs`, `lib/sprucex.d.ts`

Quick Start

<script src="/dist/sprucex.js"></script>

<div sx-data="{ count: 0 }">
  <button sx-on:click="count++">+1</button>
  <span sx-text="count"></span>
</div>

Core Directives

- `sx-data`: component root state, object/function/factory-like expression
- `sx-text`: write `textContent`
- `sx-html`: write `innerHTML`
- `sx-show`: toggle display based on truthiness
- `sx-bind:<attr>`: bind arbitrary attributes
- `sx-class`: conditional classes while preserving original classes
- `sx-toggle`: toggle a boolean state key on click
- `sx-model`: two-way binding for inputs/selects/textareas
- `sx-model` modifiers: `.trim`, `.number`, `.lazy`, plus `sx-model.debounce-ms="N"`
- `sx-ref`: register elements on `$refs`
- `sx-for`: loop on `<template>`
- `sx-memo`: cache `sx-text` / `sx-html` based on dependency keys
- `sx-local`: persist component state in `localStorage`
- `sx-lazy`: defer component init until visible
- `sx-error-fallback`: fallback expression if a binding throws
- `script[sx-init-data]`: embed JSON for identifier-style `sx-data` roots

Expressions / Helpers

- `$event`: current DOM event in handlers
- `$refs`: object of `sx-ref` elements
- `$store(name)`: access global stores
- `$emit(name, detail)`: emit component-scoped custom events
- `$index`: default loop index inside `sx-for`

Lifecycle / State Notes

- State is deeply reactive, including nested objects and common array mutators.
- Component hooks supported in `sx-data`: `init()`, `mounted()`, `destroyed()`
- A `watch` object may be defined in component state to react to property changes.
- Global shared state is created with `SpruceX.store(name, value, { persist })`.
- Named data factories can be registered with `SpruceX.data(name, factory)`.

Event Handling

- `sx-on:<event>` attaches handlers
- Supported modifiers: `.prevent`, `.stop`, `.self`, `.window`, `.document`
- Keyboard modifiers use key names such as `.enter` and `.escape`

Network Directives

- Request methods: `sx-get`, `sx-post`, `sx-put`, `sx-delete`
- Triggering: `sx-trigger`, `sx-trigger-debounce`
- Targeting and swaps: `sx-target`, `sx-swap`
- URL/body/header helpers: `sx-vars`, `sx-body`, `sx-body-type`, `sx-headers`, `sx-include`
- Request state targets: `sx-json-into`, `sx-loading-into`, `sx-error-into`
- UX helpers: `sx-disable-while-request`, `sx-text-while-request`, `sx-confirm`
- Request flow: `sx-optimistic`, `sx-revert-on-error`, `sx-poll`, `sx-poll-while`
- Cancellation: `sx-cancel-previous`

Request Notes

- Default trigger is `click`, or `submit` on forms.
- `sx-trigger` also supports inline debounce syntax such as `input.debounce.300`.
- Supported swap modes: `innerHTML`, `outerHTML`, `before`, `after`, `prepend`, `append`, `morph`
- `sx-cancel-previous` aborts only the previous in-flight request for the same binding.
- Aborted cancelable requests do not emit error events, do not write `sx-error-into`,
  and do not run `sx-revert-on-error`.
- Cancelable requests are also aborted during component refresh and destroy.

Navigation / Morphing

- `sx-page`: mark the SPA navigation container
- `sx-boost`: prefetch links on hover/focus
- `sx-boost-on`: customize boost events
- `sx-key`: preserve identity during list diffs and morphs
- `sx-preserve`: skip morphing for a specific element
- `SpruceX.navigate(url)`: navigate via SPA page swapping
- `SpruceX.prefetch(url)`: prefetch a page into cache
- `SpruceX.clearCache()`: clear the page cache
- `SpruceX.morph(target, source)`: morph DOM content in place

Built-In Integrations

- `sx-animate`: auto-animate list/container children
- `sx-chart`, `sx-chart-type`, `sx-chart-options`: Chart.js integration
- `sx-gridstack`, `sx-gridstack-options`, `sx-gridstack-option:<name>`: GridStack integration
- `sx-gridstack-on-change`, `sx-gridstack-on-added`, `sx-gridstack-on-removed`,
  `sx-gridstack-on-dragstop`, `sx-gridstack-on-resizestop`: write GridStack events into state

Animation / Boot Notes

- `sx-animate` requires the Auto Animate library to be loaded separately unless
  you wire it through `SpruceX.setAutoAnimate(...)`.
- `window.SpruceXBoot.initTheme()` runs once before component auto-init.
- `SpruceX.animate(el, options)` manually enables auto-animate on a DOM element.

JavaScript API

- `SpruceX.init()`
- `SpruceX.store(name, value, { persist })`
- `SpruceX.data(name, factory)`
- `SpruceX.inspect()`
- `SpruceX.navigate(url)`
- `SpruceX.prefetch(url)`
- `SpruceX.clearCache()`
- `SpruceX.morph(target, source)`
- `SpruceX.animate(el, options)`
- `SpruceX.setAutoAnimate(autoAnimate)`
- `SpruceX.integration(name, integration)`

Plugin Authoring

SpruceX plugins are integrations registered with:
`SpruceX.integration(name, integration)`

Hooks

1) `setup(component)` optional one-time setup per component instance
2) `scan(component, el)` optional element scan hook used to collect bindings
3) `update(component)` optional render/update hook
4) `teardown(component)` optional cleanup hook

Useful component helpers

- `component.evaluateExpressionOrLiteral(raw)`
- `component.assignStateValue(targetExpr, value)`
- `component.state`
- `component.root`

Minimal plugin example

<script>
  SpruceX.integration("uppercase", {
    setup(component) {
      component._uppercaseBindings = [];
    },
    scan(component, el) {
      const expr = el.getAttribute("sx-uppercase");
      if (!expr) return;
      component._uppercaseBindings.push({ el, expr });
    },
    update(component) {
      (component._uppercaseBindings || []).forEach(({ el, expr }) => {
        const value = component.evaluateExpressionOrLiteral(expr);
        el.textContent = String(value ?? "").toUpperCase();
      });
    },
    teardown(component) {
      component._uppercaseBindings = [];
    },
  });
</script>

<div sx-data="{ name: 'sprucex' }">
  <span sx-uppercase="name"></span>
</div>
SpruceX

Blueprint-grade reactive markup tooling.
